name: Keftes De Prassa (Leek Meatballs)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Dill weed or 2 teaspoons Dried dill weed
Lemon wedges or Red wine vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the roots from the bulb end of the leeks. Remove the tough outer leaves and trim off the tops.
2. Slice lengthwise and wash thoroughly under running water. Slice into 1/2-inch pieces.
3. Bring salted water to a boil, add the leeks, and simmer for 10 minutes or until tender. Drain in a colander and squeeze out all water.
4. Combine the remaining ingredients (except flour and oil), add the drained leeks, and mix gently.
5. Shape into meatballs and roll in flour. Flatten the meatballs slightly.
6. Fry in 1/4-inch oil until brown on both sides. Add more oil to the pan as needed.
7. Serve with lemon wedges or red wine vinegar.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings.
Variations: Substitute 5 cups finely chopped parsley or scallions for the leeks. The beef mixture can be shaped into finger rolls, stuffed with cooked rice, and fried in olive or sesame oil. Or barbecue the meatballs after coating them with flour.
